desc:
Most of the trees in this area are covered with a greyish silvery bark. A few
willows and orphaned oaks can also be seen here and there, looking small and
lonely like children without parents. The endless rows of trees continue in all
directions, inhibiting vision of the surrounding landscape, though farther to
the east the forest does seem to thin out. A faint sound of running water can be
heard north of here; the source of it is likely to be a small river.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
The tall trees here almost obscure the sky, however glimpses can be seen beyond
the massive branches. Small shrubs decorate the area, and many types of
sweet-smelling berries can be found growing here in a variety of colours. Here
and there footprints can be seen leading in various directions through the
forest. To the north the mighty Celebrant can be seen.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The tall trunks of mellyrn rise all around. Their leaves, blowing gently in the
wind, glimmer slightly as they waft from side to side, dazzling the senses of
any passers-by. A large tree has fallen here, blocking any passage to the east.
The forest continues to the west, and to the south a clearing in the forest can
be seen upon a small hill. The river Celebrant lies to the north.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A large mallorn has fallen down to the west here, blocking all passage in that
direction. Except for the fallen tree which has lost its shine, the rest of the
area glows with a slight golden tinge, illuminating the surroundings with
wonder. The forest path runs east and south, where it continues on through the
woodlands. The Celebrant can be heard flowing in the north.
name: At a Fallen Tree

desc:
Large mellyrn grow all around the path, reaching for the sky with their slender
branches. Bathing the forest in a pale golden light, they seem to span the
woodlands as far as the eye can see. A sense of peace permeates the air. The
forest path continues to the south and east, while to the west a large fallen
tree can be seen. A wide river runs just north of here.
name: Path in the Forest

desc:
Mellyrn surround the path, rising high above the tops of other, smaller trees.
Numerous footprints in the soft ground indicate that the forest is inhabited by
many different creatures. The ground by the bases of the trees is fertile and
the growth wild, aiding these forest dwellers with nourishment. The path runs
east-west.
name: Path in the Forest

desc:
A small forest path begins here and heads west, deeper into the forest. Three
small white stones mark its outset. It winds among the mellyrn, offering a
breathtaking view of the surrounding forest. Off the path, a small, clean stream
can be seen, winding its way southwest through the trees. The river Celebrant
can be seen to the north. The forest stretches out in all directions.
name: Beginning of the Forest Path

desc:
A pale golden light shines from the leaves and branches of the trees, bathing
the area with a radiant sense of tranquility. The atmosphere here is very
calming, and even the animals and birds seem to be affected by it. All sounds of
wildlife appear to be slightly muted, as if the woodland creatures did not wish
to disturb the peace of the forest. The forest continues to the west, south and
east. To the north you can see a large river.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A pale golden light emanates from the crowns of the great mellyrn. The other
trees and the growth on the forest floor bathe in the soft warm radiance. Tracks
of small woodland animals can be seen by the base of the trees. The forest
continues in all directions save north, where the Celebrant can be heard flowing
swiftly.
name: Light Forest

desc:
If any travellers ever passed through this forest, their passage does not seem
to have disturbed its tranquil peace. The local wildlife and the trees seem to
co-exist perfectly, neither one disturbing the presence of the other. The golden
forest continues in all directions but north, the glow of the mellyrn fading
slightly to the south.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The forest continues in all directions. Its great trees stretch the branches
towards the sky. The mellyrn appear to shine lightly here, but the soft glow
fades and ultimately disappears to the south, where the forest thins out
slightly. To the east and west, the forest continues unchanged. The Celebrant
can be seen to the north.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The air in this light forest is fresh and pleasant. On this side of the
Celebrant, the silvery mellyrn grow shorter and more sparsely than farther into
the forest. Here and there you catch a glimpse of gold behind the silver-grey
tree trunks. Low, soft grass and undergrowth flourishes on the ground.
name: Spacious Forest

desc:
The forest opens up to a small, secluded clearing where trees give way to low
bushes. Daylight reaches the level ground here, lighting the clearing in daytime
and playing on the silvery and golden trees of the mallorn forest. A winding
path starts from here and runs eastwards, picking its way through the spacious
woods. The sound of rushing water can be heard from the north, where the swift
Celebrant lies.
name: Forest Clearing

desc:
Among the silver-grey trunks of the ancient trees a winding path runs through
this forest. The mellyrn are covered in a lush foliage which flickers in golden
shades as an occasional gust of wind rustles the leaves. The path leads
eastwards following the edge of the wood.
name: On a Winding Path

desc:
This path runs east and west, circling onward around the old, silver-grey trees.
Here it follows tightly the channel of the rapid Celebrant which runs north from
here. The path seems somewhat used; debris and dry leaves cover it, but it is
broad and not overgrown. Southwards the vegetation thins out, and the mellyrn
give way to other trees.
name: Path in the Mallorn Forest

desc:
At this place the path becomes faint and seems to end quite abruptly. The ground
sinks, forming a small basin. The trees cling together; their thick trunks tower
around and their many branches arch high above you. The forest floor seems to be
lit by the elusive glow of the mallorn-trees.
name: Path in the Mallorn Forest

desc:
Some trees seem to have fallen on the edge of the forest here, perhaps having
been struck by a storm or some other force. Because of the increased amount of
light, undergrowth has quickly taken over this area, and even some flowers can
be seen growing between the tree-trunks that lie on the ground. Short, thick
stumps lie about, already growing light moss. One stump appears devoid of any
undergrowth at all, as though the forest itself were recoiling from its presence
here.
name: Stumps in the Forest

desc:
A carpet of sparse, stunted grass and weeds covers this rolling plateau.
Occasionally patches of green, healthier-looking grass mix in with their more
common cousins. Plains continue in all directions, and a dried-up streambed can
be seen to the west.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
A shallow channel of an old stream furrows the flat plains here. The source of
this river had obviously run dry long ago and although the grass on the partly
crumbled banks of the streambed still grows quite densely, the ground around it
is barren and covered by the carpet of dry grass.
name: Dried-up Stream

desc:
The arid lands stretch out, growing little here but low yellow-brown grasses and
occasional clumps of taller, green grass. Nothing rises from the level
landscape, nothing casts a single shadow, and travelling here can be quite
unpleasant in the summer. An old streambed can be seen crossing through the
plains in the east.
name: Deserted Plains

desc:
The sandy, dry ground has a yellow-brown hue here and vegetation is very sparse.
The desolation continues as far west as the eye can see. In the east the ground
slopes slightly and some big rocks can be seen on the horizon. No kind of
wildlife can be seen here, and an oppressive silence prevails.
name: Above the Valley

desc:
Here and there small cracks cut through the yellow soil. The plateau is open and
flat, but westwards the ground rises and some hills can be made out in the
distance. The air is still and quiet. Tufts of tough yellow grass grow sparsely
in the dry earth.
name: Lifeless Plains

desc:
An oppressive silence prevails in this grim part of the desolation. Living
things do not seem to come here very often and the air feels heavy and seems to
hush all sounds, as though the land itself did not wish to be disturbed. The dry
ground seems to deaden even the sounds of footsteps.
name: Silent Plains

desc:
The lands here are barren, as though the life in this area were in hibernation.
A few sparse shrubs can be seen scattered about the landscape, providing barely
any shelter or sustenance for any creature. Plains can be seen to the west and
east, while a low hill can be seen to the north.
name: Arid Grasslands

desc:
A small hilltop graces the otherwise unimpressive landscape here. The vegetation
is poor, consisting mostly of stunted grass and the occasional withered shrub.
Downhill to the west the terrain becomes rocky, while the plains continue in all
other directions.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
The terrain becomes more rocky here, and some large boulders lie about providing
some shelter from the winds. In the middle of this immense desolation one might
wonder whether this ground has even seen a living creature before. Only the wind
is always present here, passing through the lands, licking the rocks and wiping
the occasional loose stones away.
name: Windy Plateau

desc:
The plains here are dry and barren. The grass looks dull and grey. The few trees
and bushes are half-dead and the wildlife seems to be non-existent. A few small
hills mark the landscape. The hills continue to the north, while the land to the
west appears to be flat. To the east the terrain looks more rocky.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
These flat plains are filled with tall, lush grass, yellow and white flowers,
some shrubbery and a few trees. Actually it is quite easy to hide in the grass,
but on the other hand, your worst enemy could be hidden there as well. The
plains stretches before you in all directions.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
The even landscape is covered with lush grass. There are no hills, a few trees
and some scattered shrubbery. Judging by the noise, the wildlife here is rich
and consists mostly of birds, frogs and locusts. Plains fill the view in all
directions, but one thing is more dominant in the north: a great tree.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
The land is completely flat here. The ground is soft, but numerous small paths
make it easy to travel through the terrain. Thick green grass covers the ground
like a lush carpet, its pattern changing with every gust of the wind. A few
trees and bushes can be seen rising out of the tall grass. To the west and north
the plains seem to grow more muddy.
name: Wild Plains

desc:
These plains are covered in thick grass and low shrubs interspersed by
occasional bushes and trees. The ground is soft and muddy, making it hard to
travel across the terrain on foot. To the west a fogbank can be spotted on the
horizon. Grasslands dominate the view to the north, east and south.
name: Muddy Plains

desc:
Most of the ground is covered by water. Thick vegetation covers the spots where
the ground rises above the water level. It would be hard to travel in one
consistent direction through this terrain, and the fog that covers the land
would not make it easier. The fog seems to grow in density to the west, while
the area to the east appears more friendly.
name: Swampy Grasslands

desc:
In the middle of the marshes, where the water seems to take control, a large
plateau rises suddenly out of the swamp. The ground is solid and covered by
grass. The fog seems to clear a bit, but the surroundings are still the same:
dirty brown water, knolls and smaller plateaus of earth and grass with plenty of
swarming insects.
name: The Plateau

desc:
The fog is dense here, so dense that you can barely see your feet. The ground is
covered with water, mostly about one foot deep, but in some places apparently
deeper and maybe even bottomless. The fog seems to drop in density to the west
while it looks impervious in the other directions. There is no wildlife to be
seen here, but the swamp makes its own sounds.
name: The Swamp

desc:
Dark and dirty water dominates here, but small plateaus and knolls of earth and
grass make it possible to move between the narrow paths. The marshes seem to
continue to the north and east, judging by the fog that covers the ground in
these directions. To the west you can leave the marshes.
name: Marshy Grasslands

desc:
The fields are covered by grass and water. The ground is soft and muddy from the
water of a small brook that flows out of the side of a tiny hill. Surrounded by
small bushes and shrubs, the brook runs north. To the east the land is covered
in fog; in all other directions, the grasslands continue.
name: Wet Fields

desc:
Much like the surrounding areas, this part of the plains is covered by a soft
layer of grass interspersed by small plants. On the horizon, in the distant
north, the shape of a large tree is visible. Also, in that direction the soil
becomes drier and the grassy cover thins. To the east, in a stark contrast to
the dry areas directly north of here, lies a patch of damp swampy ground.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A brownish grass with a short stem and prickly tip dominates this area. Except
for the struggling grass few other plants have been able to grow in the dry,
hard soil. To the north, where the soil contains more moisture and the grass is
greener and thicker, a large tree can be seen standing lonely on the plains.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
The fields are covered by an uneven carpet of grass. Below the grass, the ground
is soft and muddy. Shallow puddles of water can be spotted here and there. A
small brook is coming from the south and continuing north. A few low bushes and
trees stand near the brook. To the north, west and south, the grassy plains
continue. To the east, the land is covered in fog.
name: Muddy Fields

desc:
Brownish water is all over, with grass and small knolls of earth rising above
its surface. The air is filled with fog which makes it hard to see far, but
small paths make it possible to move around. The marshes spread out to the
north, east and south. To the west you can move out from the fog, while it gets
more dense to the east.
name: Marshy Grasslands

desc:
The water is about one foot deep here. This appears to be the centre of the
swamp, although it is hard to tell because of the heavy fog that covers the area
in all directions. The fog seems to be less dense to the east. Some strange
noises penetrate the fog; their source is apparently the swamp itself, since no
wildlife can be seen anywhere.
name: The Swamp

desc:
The ground is very muddy. Most of it is covered by dark and dirty water which
often makes it hard to even see if there is any solid bottom below. The fogbank
is dense but still growing in density to the west. To the east and north the
terrain seem less severe. The air is filled with the buzzing of insects.
name: Swampy Grasslands

desc:
Grass dominates the ground which is very soft from various puddles and streams
of water. Small bushes grow in clusters with smaller growth surrounding them. To
the west the water is more dominating, a heavy fog seems to cover the land in
that direction, while to the east the plains continue to the very horizon.
name: Swampy Grasslands

desc:
Small bushes and short grass are the dominating forms of vegetation in this
area. A few lonely trees stand amidst the plains, proudly reaching for the sky.
The ground beneath the grassy cover is soft and in places muddy. A large fogbank
can be spotted on the western horizon. In all other directions the plains
continue.
name: Muddy Plains

desc:
The grass grows thick and tall in these plains. No source of water can be seen,
yet the soil is moist and even muddy in some spots. Some large footprints can be
seen caked into some dry mud, and there is a distinct animal smell coming from
somewhere nearby. The terrain to the north seems to be covered in a grey dust.
name: Muddy Field

desc:
One living thing is dominant here: a great mallorn. With its smooth silvery-grey
bark and its magnificent crown it stands, proudly reaching for the sky, lonely
and obviously a wild seed from a mallorn of Lorien. Around it grass grows
vigorously, while the mallorn stands like a guardian, spreading its branches
like wings. To the north the air appears to be dark grey, and no sounds of
wildlife come from that direction.
name: The Huge Tree

desc:
A small pond is here, almost hidden in grass and vegetation. The water seems
clear and refreshing, and the growth around the pond is rich and vigorous, which
also can be said about the wildlife here. Plains can be seen stretching to the
horizon in all directions except to the northwest where a heavy grey dust seems
to cover the land. The wildlife seems to avoid that area.
name: Small Pond

desc:
The soil in this part of the plains is dry and barren, with many cracks crossing
its parched crust. The grass is rather yellowish and dead-looking, and there are
spots where even this grass refuses to grow. Occasional trees and shrubs look as
lifeless as the grass. A few small hills mark the landscape to the south, while
the terrain in other directions looks more flat and vigorous.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The smooth, dry grasslands continue onwards, never making a sudden rise or
descent. The years seem to have worn off the edges of this land; whatever hills
were once present now lie smooth, bland and desolated, a tribute to the
never-ending power of wind and rain.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
This, a higher point in the landscape, is less protected from the strong winds,
which make progress sometimes quite difficult. The air is the only thing in
motion here, sweeping over the hills and through the valleys. Only grass can be
seen for several miles in all directions, swaying slowly in the wind.
name: Windswept Point

desc:
A small hill has formed here, slightly elevated from the rest of the surrounding
landscape but not high enough to provide much of a view. The ground slopes
downward to the east. To the west the land becomes much more barren, while
grasslands can be seen to the south.
name: On a Flat Hilltop

desc:
The ground slopes steeply westwards here, but the climb is fairly easy. The
ground is firm and bare due to the constant winds which have blown away most of
the loose soil. The hill in the west seems the highest point in the landscape,
but doesn't seem high enough to provide much of a view.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
The ground is hard and cracked in places, and even though life seems completely
impossible in such a desolation, some persistent grasses can be seen pushing
through the soil. The even ground rises towards a high hill which stands west
from here. At this point travelling is quite comfortable as the slope is not
very steep yet.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
This arid plateau slopes descending to the east towards a valley. A dry
streambed can be seen to the east, running north-south, like a large snake
traversing the land. Here the landscape is desert-like, the sandy soil growing
only some sparse tufts of dry yellow grass.
name: Above the Valley

desc:
The ground descends towards the valley in the northeast. The slope is barren and
dry, and only short grass thrives in the unfertile soil. Here and there patches
of bare brown soil dot the carpet of yellowy grass. Below, in the north, an
ancient mill can be seen by the long-dried stream.
name: Barren Slope

desc:
Running north and south the dry riverbed crosses the plains. On the banks grow
tufts of dry grass, and in places the channel has crumbled and the slides make
it easy to cross. Once, long ago, it had perhaps been a swift river, but now it
is little but a shallow track in the middle of these endless plains.
name: Dried-up Stream

desc:
Small tufts of grass are the predominant flora over these bleak, silent plains,
interrupted only occasionally by some higher grass or a small shrub or two.
Little wildlife can be heard; this lands only faithful visitor is the wind. To
the southeast some large rocks can be seen in the distance. The plains continue
in all directions.
name: Quiet Plains

desc:
At the border of the forest in the north the faint scent of the plants of the
Golden Wood can be sensed. Here the smell of woods hangs in the air and the
forest consists mostly of various broadleaf trees, but farther in the woods one
can catch a glimpse of the silvery trunks of the mellyrn.
name: On the Fringes of the Forest

desc:
Thinning southwards the forest is a mixture of both mellyrn and other plants of
the Golden Wood and ordinary, though healthy and beautiful, vegetation. Even
here the Elven plants seem to radiate a faint, special glow but the effect is
more noticeable in the north. In all other directions the forest ends and level
grasslands take place.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
The mellyrn grow thicker here than in the surrounding area. The trees lean to
each other, their many branches reaching for the sunlight, intertwining and
forming a solid canopy of leaves. At the dim forest floor some plants seem to
reflect a faint, golden light revealing the rich and varying undergrowth of many
plants, grasses and moss. To the north a forest clearing can be seen.
name: Dim Forest

desc:
On the outskirts of this forest, the vegetation grows lighter and the ground
becomes less moist. Under the trees low shrubbery covers the ground. The
undergrowth is easy to walk in, but becomes heavier in the north. Southwards, an
expanse of grasslands extends into the distance.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
The trees here grow more sparsely than to the north, yet their stature is still
breathtaking. A few mellyrn are scattered among the other trees. The golden
trees appear to be the source of a faint radiance which grows stronger in the
north. The forest continues to the north, east and west, while to the south it
opens up into the plains.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Here mellyrn and other trees seem to blend perfectly. The light golden glow of
the mellyrn and the fragrant smell of pines pervade the senses. The forest
border is evident to the south, as almost no animal tracks lead in that
direction. The forest continues to the north, east and west.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Draughts of air move quietly through the forest, circling around the still
branches of the massive trees overhead. A peaceful, serene atmosphere reigns
here, disturbed only by the footfalls of the occasional traveller, passing by on
the way to a faraway destination. At the foot of a large tree on the south side,
tall grass seems to conceal something. The forest continues to the north, east
and west, while rocks and trees make southern passage impossible.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Tall golden mellyrn tower above the ground. Mixing with them are other kinds of
trees: firs, pines and a few rowans bearing clusters of red berries. Many shrubs
and bushes grow by the base of the trees, providing a source of nourishment for
the local wildlife.
name: Light Forest

desc:
In this part of the forest mellyrn mingle with the ordinary trees. Although the
golden trees are fairer than the others, all of the trees here are astonishing,
seemingly beyond the trees of any other forest in beauty and stature. It is
possible to continue to the north, south and east, but to the west the forest
grows too dense, making passage impossible.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A path can be seen running through the forest to the north. On the sides of the
path tall mellyrn raise their branches to the sky. To the south the
golden-crowned trees of Lothlorien seem to dwindle, giving way to trees with
crowns of green. The fresh, sharp smell of pine needles is in the air. The dense
forest makes passage to the east impossible.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The small forest path winds its way through the dense forest. Judging by the
animal tracks by the sides of the path wildlife flourishes here. The path
continues to the west, winding through the mixed forest, and to the north where
it heads towards a tall stand of mellyrn. To the south, more tall trees can be
seen.
name: Path in the Forest

desc:
The forest stretches out all around. Its great trees tower majestically above
the ground. A small clearing can be seen to the west, where a low slope rises
gently into the distance. To the north, a few fallen logs can be seen scattered
across the forest floor. Beyond them, the glimmer of mellyrn rises into the sky
like a drop of the morning sun. A small forest path leads east.
name: Path in the Forest

desc:
Lying on a small hill, this clearing offers a good view of the surrounding
forest. To the north the crowns of the mellyrn dot the horizon like sparks of
golden light. A small forest path runs down the slope of the hill to the east. A
heady scent of flowers is in the air.
name: Clearing in the Forest

desc:
A light wind whispers softly in the branches of the trees. A sweet, fresh smell
of foliage permeates the air. The scent seems to fade to the east where a small
clearing in the forest can be seen upon a low hill. Judging by the occasional
animal sounds and the tracks on the ground, there is a lot of animal activity in
this part of the great forest.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The trees in this part of the forest are mostly slender, grey-barked mellyrn.
Thriving in the moist, fertile ground, the grass under the trees is plentiful
and thick. High-pitched insect sounds can be heard, although no insects are
visible. To the north the forest grows more dense. To the south the grassy
fields seem to continue forever.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
This area is covered by rich grasses that surround a single, extremely tall,
tree. The tree, which rises majestically above the plains, is covered by a thick
bark that has many knots on the surface. Many of the lower branches of the tree
can be seen strutting out and are probably capable of supporting the weight of a
heavy troll.
name: A Very Tall Tree

desc:
The flat plains are covered in tall grass. The lush grass conceals soft and
muddy ground below. A small brook can be spotted running through the plains, its
clear waters flowing from the south and turning east towards the plains covered
in a grey mist. A single large tree can be seen to the west and some hills are
visible in the north, but the grassy plains dominate the view.
name: Muddy Plains

desc:
The ground here is soft and muddy, covered by puddles of water that grow in size
to the southeast. A traveller on foot would find it nearly impossible to walk
through this terrain without stepping into the dark water. A dim fogbank starts
here and continues to the east and south through the swampy lands. Drier fields
and plains stretch out to the west and north.
name: Edge of the Marshes

desc:
Water dominates the area, the soft ground about one foot below its surface. In
some places small bushes and other vegetation grow on drier elevations, but
since the area is covered in fog, it is hard to see where these spots are. The
fog seems to clear to the north and west while it gets denser to the south.
There is little wildlife, but occasional movement can be seen or felt in the
dirty water.
name: Swampy Plains

desc:
This part of the plains is dominated by water and grass. The ground is soft and
muddy. Small bushes grow here, but there is no larger growth. According to the
humming and buzzing noises, this area is inhabited by a multitude of insects. A
light fog covers the ground. To the south and west the fog gets much thicker as
the grassland turns into a swamp.
name: Swampy Ground

desc:
The soft, muddy ground is mostly bare in this part of the plains. Puddles of
water of different sizes can be seen everywhere. The only growth is some thorny
shrubbery. The southwestern horizon is filled with a heavy grey fog which might
indicate the presence of a marsh. In all other directions, plains and fields
spread out.
name: Muddy Ground

desc:
In the middle of the monotonous plains lies a small field with vigorous grass
growing beautifully along with many yellow and purple flowers. Around the field,
the plains can be seen stretching out in all directions. On the southwestern
horizon a large fogbank can be spotted and to the east a grey dust cloud covers
the area.
name: Small Field

desc:
The ground is rough and black and the grass is scorched. Nothing lives here and
nothing grows here. The air is heavy and hard to breathe from the dust filling
it. The silence fills every good soul with sorrow and every evil soul with hope.
The sombre atmosphere absorbs any noise. It is hard to determine whether this is
an old burial site or whether the strange aura of this place is due to something
else.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
This area is grey and black, and the air is heavy to breathe because of all the
dust in it. The ground is rough and black; the grass is scorched. Nothing lives
here and nothing grows here. Some might want to scream, but it is impossible to
even say a word. The sombre atmosphere absorbs all sound. This could be an old
burial place, or maybe it is just the way of nature, or maybe something else
caused this.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
The tall, lush grass is dominant here, but a large path has been trampled
through it by the passage of many animals. The path leads from the north to the
south. Some tracks lead away from the path to the east, but none lead west where
the horizon is covered with a heavy, grey dust cloud.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
Tall, vigorous grass is dominant here. The land is flat, but a few small hills
can be seen on the southern horizon. The wildlife is rich, according to the
sounds and the occasional matted patches of grass. A few groups of trees mark
the otherwise featureless landscape. The plains continue in all directions, but
to the south the terrain becomes drier.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Almost no signs of wildlife present themselves here, save the occasional bird
circling in the sky, probably on its way to a more lively environment. The
carpet of grass continues, following the gently varying contours of the
landscape which continues in every direction.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
As one progresses southwards, the grass becomes more sparse and the soil more
barren, indicating a drier climate. It seems that rainfall is not common in this
part of the plains. Some species seem to have adapted to this environment,
however; these plains are not entirely lifeless.
name: Arid Grasslands

desc:
Rising southwards the ground slopes steeply forming a low hill. A narrow path
runs through the grassy slope, winding around clumps of shrubbery, avoiding the
occasional holes in the ground and leading towards the hilltop in the south.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
The ground rises southwest towards a flat, grassy hilltop which seems to be the
highest point in the surrounding areas. From here the visibility is already good
to the north and east, over the bland rolling plateau which extends for miles.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
A shallow, winding ditch cuts through the ground here like a scar, its banks
partly crumbled in. The ground is barren, but the ditch seems to provide shelter
for some patches of low, tough grass. Dirt and withered grass cover the bottom
of the ditch.
name: By a Ditch

desc:
A small valley cuts the plateau to the east where an old, ruined building can be
seen next to a dried streambed. To the south the ground descends more gently.
Much time has passed since these fields were last inhabited. Now, the area is
dry and abandoned; rarely does a traveller pass through this place.
name: Rocky Bluff

desc:
Though the stream in the east has not brought water to the valley for ages the
stony walls of an old watermill still stand here, surrounded by the rolling
fields. The building has survived the years well, and though the roof has partly
collapsed the walls and the channel which once led water to the wheel have not
crumbled yet. A stony doorway leads in to the upper floor of the mill, but from
here it is not possible to see what lies inside.
name: By an Old Mill

desc:
The stream has been quite narrow here, as this channel is only about five yards
wide. It is deeper, though, but not difficult to cross, as the sandy banks have
mostly crumbled and are easy to climb. Small tufts of grass can be found growing
on the ruined banks.
name: Dry Streambed

desc:
Grasslands extend in all directions here, open and flat except to the west where
the land slopes gently towards a shallow valley floor. A dry streambed can be
seen in that direction; a river must once have run through this area. Some
ruined buildings can also be seen to the north and west.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
The terrain here might have once been a part of a local farm, as it is open and
very level. Now, however, the soil is barren and unfertile, allowing hardly
anything to grow. These fields appear to have been ruined long ago.
name: Ruined Fields

desc:
The riverbed bends here, going west and south and passing some ruined
foundations of farm buildings which lie in the north. The banks are higher and
steeper here, but it would not be very difficult to cross this channel. While
the grass reigns on the banks, not much grows on the shadowy bottom.
name: Bending Streambed

desc:
The streambed runs east and north here, descending from the hills in the north
toward the desolate valley. The banks are crumbled and overgrown, but not much
grows in the shadows of the dry, sandy bottom. The stream obviously dried up a
long time ago. Some bleached bones can be seen resting against the eastern bank
of the riverbed.
name: Bending Streambed

desc:
The bluff has partly crumbled here, creating small potholes in the ground. To
the north an old path descends gently toward a shallow valley. The path seems to
cross the valley floor and continue to the other side in the distance. Some
large boulders lie to the west, making the passage uphill difficult.
name: Crumbling Bluff

desc:
Hard, rough boulders lie about, blocking passage over this slope in some places.
The rocky edges seem very sharp, making passage difficult, and loose debris dots
the ground along the surface of the slope. Farther downhill the terrain becomes
harder to navigate; passage back uphill would be tiring.
name: Blocked Slope

desc:
Not much moves between this dry, cracked ground and the high, open sky. Only an
occasional bird can be seen circling high above you. The silence is surprising,
almost oppressive. The land is very flat, providing little cover to any
passers-by from wind, rain or the watchful eyes of those who would see.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
Ever on, the grasslands continue. An occasional gust of wind caresses your skin
and goes away as suddenly as it came. But for the wind the landscape is lone and
quiet; wildlife has not spread to such conditions as these. Here the ground
begins to rise slowly, climbing towards the hilltop in the south.
name: Barren Lands

desc:
The silence that prevails here is almost oppressive. No sounds of wildlife or
birds can be heard, only an occasional rustle as the wind creates waves in the
sea of grass. The grasslands continue in all directions; very little activity
can be discerned among them from here.
name: Quiet Plains

desc:
The silent plains stretch out here. In the ground, one can see same tracks
indicating that there is some wildlife here. The plateau reaches on in all
directions. Several shapes appear to be moving in the distance; but in a blink,
the land seems completely still again.
name: Quiet Plains

desc:
Tall, lush grass covers the ground completely. Only a few trees rise above the
grassy cover. Judging by the noises, the wildlife appears to be rich; the grass
is tall enough to conceal any small animals. Plains seem to continue in all
directions.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A path runs through the tall, lush grass from the north to the south. The path
widens as it runs south and is joined by many small paths coming from the east
and west. The plains continue in all directions. Groups of trees and patches of
shrubbery are the only landmarks in this level terrain.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
The plains are covered with grass, yellow, white and red flowers, bushes and
occasional trees. The land is flat; not a single hilltop can be seen. The soil
is soft, as if it has rained recently. The wildlife is rich, according to the
sounds; birds are singing, and frogs and locusts can be heard as well. The
plains continue in all directions, but a grey dust covers the land to the south.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
The tall grass is dominant here, but it looks as if a pack of animals or a big
party passed here recently because the grass is trampled in many places. The
tracks continue in the western direction, but it is hard to see where they are
going or coming from. The plains continue in all directions, but the land to the
south is covered with grey dust.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
Tall, lush grass covers the ground here. A large patch of the grass lies flat on
the ground as if many animals have trampled it down. The trampled grass seems to
run east-west. The grassy plains extend in all directions, but the southeast is
covered by a grey dust cloud, and to the north, the outline of a ruined building
can be spotted.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
Lush fields covered with vigorous grasses dominate the view. Bushes and smaller
shrubs serve as landmarks in this terrain where few trees can be spotted.
Judging by frequent animal sounds, the wildlife prospers in this area. Many
animals have trampled a path through the grass; the path continues north and
east. A large fogbank can be seen to the southwest.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
Thick wet grass dominates these plains, with a few bushes and some smaller
plants sporadically making their appearance. Here and there lonely trees can be
seen standing tall and proud. Judging by the tracks in the grass, the wildlife
consists of birds, small animals and maybe a few deer. To the south a large
fogbank can be seen covering the land, while in the other directions plains and
fields stretch out to the very horizon.
name: Wet Grass

desc:
Lush grass, small bushes and even smaller shrubs cover these plains. Lonely
trees can be seen reaching for the sky here and there. Unseen birds fill the air
with their melodious singing. These surroundings continue to the horizon in all
directions except to the south, where a dense bank of fog covers the land making
it impossible to see far.
name: Wet Plains

desc:
Covered with water and grass, the ground is soft and muddy. Small bushes and
trees grow all around. To the north, east and west the plains continue, but to
the south and southeast the marshes begin, covered in fog. To the west you can
see a few hills in the horizon.
name: Wet Plains

desc:
Tall grass interspersed with small bushes covers the ground. The stems move
slightly, indicating that living beings lurk underneath, but there is no way to
tell what creatures those might be. A few small hills can be seen on the
northern horizon; in all other directions the plains continue. The plains to the
southeast are covered in fog.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
A number of bushes grow in groups of three and four on the slightly undulating
ground that is covered by thick layers of grasses. Some of the taller grasses
sway whenever there is a light breeze, which is common to this section of the
vast plains of Lorien. To the northwest there is a hill that might offer a clear
view of this region, while to the south a tall tree stands alone.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
This is a region of rich, fruitful grassland that supports a large variety of
plants and shrubs. To the west the terrain slopes gently up towards the top of a
small hill. In all the other directions the vast grasslands disappear into the
distance.
name: East of a Small Hill

desc:
Grass dominates these plains, its even cover sometimes broken by a lonely shrub
or a cluster of bushes. A small hill rises above the plains to the north,
contrasting sharply with the seemingly endless flatlands that continue in all
other directions.
name: Near a Hill

desc:
Grasslands continue in all directions except to the northwest, where a small
hill can be made out in the distance. To the east the grass appears to be
thinner and somewhat trampled. The sweet fragrance of flowers and leaves wafts
in hazily from the north where the forest of Lothlorien lies.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
The grass is worn and trampled here, as this spot is obviously a part of a
migration route for the local wildlife. The animal route continues to the south
and east, while more grasslands can be seen to the north and west. The land is
very flat here, providing little cover from the predators, and yet wildlife
seems to prosper in this area.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
Several small bushes and other low lying vegetation can be seen among the wild
and lush grasses of these plains. Many animals or travellers seem to have passed
here, causing many trampled and worn spots in the grass. The path of trampled
grass continues to the east and west.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
A sheer rock wall, fashioned over thousands of years by the climate, blocks
passage to the north here, providing shelter for many insects and possibly also
small animals in its nooks and crannies. A small ruin can be seen to the east. A
path meanders its way through the grasslands to the south and west.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
An ancient ruin of a stone building stands here. The remainders of the walls and
the floor are overgrown with grass and trailing plants, but in the centre of the
ruin nothing grows. To the north, the great forest of Lothlorien can be seen in
the distance. The grasslands extend in all directions.
name: A Small Ruin

desc:
A small stand of trees grows here. The trees look almost as if they were envious
of the breathtaking forest of Lothlorien to the north. In the centre of the
stand of trees a grassy clearing houses a small pond, the watering place for
local wildlife and a source of drinking water for a traveller through these lush
grasslands.
name: Stand of Trees

desc:
The air is full of all kinds of sounds, as small animals and birds go about
their ways, uninhibited by travellers through these lands. A small stand of
trees can be seen to the west, dwarfed by the large forest to the north. Plains
dominate the view in all directions, but to the east the grass appears to grow
less vigorously.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
A small, animal-made path runs north and south here. There are many tracks
leading in other directions as well, as the area's residents passed here
searching for food, water and shelter. Grasslands continue in all directions. In
the north, the great forest of Lothlorien looms in the distance.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
This vast grassy plain is apparently home to many different types of creatures.
Animal tracks and holes in the ground are everywhere. Small areas of foliage and
trees can be seen here and there, providing sustenance and homes for small and
large animals alike, as well as rest for the weary traveller. Many animal tracks
lead west of here.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
At this spot it is very quiet, and even the sound the withering grass creates
underfoot sounds very noisy. There is something oppressive in the air, as if no
sound is welcome here. Nothing seems to be moving in the lands surrounding you,
and a complete stillness prevails.
name: Quiet Plains

desc:
The ground drops here, forming a low depression where the vegetation seems a
little healthier than in the surrounding plains. Various bushes grow densely at
the bottom of the hollow, but in the shadow below them the vegetation is not as
rich, and the roots of the bushes dominate the ground area. The depression
appears to provide home for a few small animals as well.
name: Small Depression

desc:
Gently rolling the landscape goes on, the carpet of grass following the contours
of the soil. Gusts of wind come and go, keeping the grass slowly moving all the
time. Luckily the winds are not very strong here as the low shrubbery would not
provide much of a shelter.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
These grasslands seem less inviting than those to the west, the ground becoming
cracked and parched. The winds gust around the small, dwarfed bushes and shrubs,
biting at the land as well as any who might be travelling upon it. The barren
lands continue in all directions except west.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
This seemingly lifeless slope falls slowly to the east, while climbing wearily
to the west. The sides of the sloping ground have been blasted by the wind for
so long that they are all but bare, yet small patches of life do exist here and
there.
name: Barren Slope

desc:
The ground slopes steadily downwards to the east, providing a good view of the
lands in that direction. The soil here is generally worn and crumbled, however
patches of somewhat fertile soil can still be seen. A dusty path runs through
the landscape north from here, descending to a small valley in the northeast.
name: Sloping Ground

desc:
The dry channel wanders north-south here through this small valley. The ground
rises gently to the north and west. The bottom of the streambed is sandy and dry
and the banks mostly crumbled. Northwards the channel becomes even more shallow,
and there seems to be an old trail crossing it.
name: Dry Streambed

desc:
An old, dusty path cuts east-west through this barren landscape. The path is
faint and worn, but still easily followed. To the east it dives into a shallow
valley; there seems to be a dry channel of a stream running through the valley
floor. Some old buildings can be seen to the east as well, but the valley seems
completely deserted.
name: Path to the Valley

desc:
Some isolated greenery attempts to eke out a meagre existence on this shallow
slope but most of the area is brown and solemn, wind-beaten over many years. A
small path can be seen at the base of the slope to the east, while barren
grasslands can be found in all other directions.
name: Barren Slope

desc:
The bare, lifeless plain stretches out, the yellow-brown colour of the soil
blending with the green, grassy spots. Only the wind is not afraid of this harsh
environment; it is a frequent visitor. Plains continue in all directions.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
Not much breaks the peace of these monotonous, quiet grasslands. An occasional
bird can be seen circling in the sky but there are no other signs of wildlife.
In the north a broad, dark green belt of shrubbery can be seen cutting through
the bland grasslands.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Some faint paths can be seen criss-crossing the grass here, made by the small
animals which live in this wilderness, and every now and then something can be
seen scurrying through the foot-long grass. The grasslands stretch out in all
directions. To the south, the ground appears to drop to form a hollow.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The land rolls gently upwards from the low basins, where the ground is a little
softer and the vegetation richer, to the bare hilltops, which provide a
monotonous view: immense grasslands continue in all directions. A variety of
grasses and plants grow here, but no wildlife is to be seen.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grass is very lush here due to the plentiful rainfall and healthy soil in
the region. Most of the wildlife seems to focus their attention on the area of
trampled soil and grass north and west of here. To the east, south and west,
plains extend to the very horizon, while the forest of Lothlorien can be seen
far to the north.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The soft soil and lush green grass is trampled from the passage of many animals,
possibly on their way to feeding or water. Many animal tracks can be seen
heading south into the plains and north towards the forest. To the east and west
the land is less trampled and the plains continue as a seemingly endless flow of
green grass.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
The fertile ground of these plains is covered with lush grass and large bushes.
A few trees can be seen standing proudly alone, stretching their branches to the
sky. The forest of Lothlorien gleams in the north, its glory but a distant
memory. Yet while the forest scents are light in the air here, the presence of
the Golden Wood can still be felt drawing the wanderer towards its eaves. More
plains can be seen in all directions. The land to the east looks well travelled.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
Rich grasslands cover the land here, stretching out in every direction. Only
occasional bushes and a few scattered trees try to break the domination of the
grass. The forest of Lothlorien can be seen to the north, towering to the sky. A
gentle golden glow seems to emanate from the treetops. At a distance to the
south a small group of trees can be seen.
name: Fertile Grasslands

desc:
The forest reaches high into the sky, inspiring awe even in the travellers
looking at it from aside. The sounds of creatures scurrying through the tall
grasses indicate that there is an abundance of life here. Small bushes dot the
grassland, providing sustenance to many of the local inhabitants.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Majestic in its appearance, the light forest on the outskirts of Lothlorien
rises in the north, obscuring most of the sky with its array of foliage. Vast
plains stretch out to the east and west, covered with a rich carpet of green. To
the south the land drops abruptly. The only way in that direction would be down
a nearly vertical cliff wall which appears impossible to scale if you cannot
fly.
name: Forest Border

desc:
The plains are dotted by low trees, mere saplings compared to their brothers and
sisters in the majestic forest to the north. Although the forest of Lothlorien
is miles away, the air is sweet with the fragrance of its trees. The grassland
continues in every direction but to the north, where a huge fire burned away
everything up to the forest border.
name: Forested Plains

desc:
The plains continue in all directions. The soft ground is lined by lush, rich
grasses. Scattered among the plains, occasional tall trees proudly reach for the
sky. Small ponds and pools dot the area. To the south, the grass appears to be
slightly trampled.
name: Forested Plains

desc:
The flatlands seem to continue endlessly to the east and south. To the west,
however, a small hill rises above the landscape, its sight unusual amidst these
level lowlands where even the smallest elevation acts as a landmark. Far to the
north a forest can be seen, its tall trees stretching up towards the sky.
name: East of a Hill

desc:
A small hill rises from the surrounding plains here. Short tussocks of grass
grow upon its top, making it look almost comical. From here one can see across
vast miles of plains in any direction. The ground on the top of the hill seems
somewhat downtrodden, and large footprints can be made out in the soil near the
top.
name: On a Small Hill

desc:
Many plants and grasses thrive upon the moist soil of this fertile land.
Numerous small flowers, as well as a few larger ones, often sprout here during
the spring, filling the land with an abundance of colour. In all directions one
could watch the vast plains roll on endlessly. A small hill to the east breaks
the monotony of this otherwise dull landscape.
name: Colourful Plains

desc:
Clumps of brittle green grass provide a stark contrast to the otherwise dry,
dusty soil that dominates this section of the plains of Lorien. A forest is
visible far to the north, while in all other directions the plains seem to roll
on endlessly. Farther to the south the land becomes less dusty as well as less
sparsely populated by the grasses.
name: Dry Plains

desc:
The plains continue in every direction but to the north, where a forest can be
seen in the distance. The ground appears to be less firm in that direction,
perhaps due to the small streams or other sources of water that nourish the
trees and the lush forest undergrowth.
name: Sparse Grasslands

desc:
A few scattered tall trees and smaller bushes, interspersed with other types of
flora, complement the lush grass bed. Grasslands stretch out in all directions,
except to the north, where the outskirts of the magnificent forest of Lothlorien
can be seen in the distance.
name: Fertile Grasslands

desc:
Lush grass grows vigorously here. It is teeming with life, moving like a living
carpet in the wilderness. Here and there beautiful flowers grow in small
clusters or all alone. The majestic forest of Lothlorien lies to the north, its
heady scents lingering in the fresh air.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Some time ago a large fire burned away everything living here. Yet when it
reached the border of Lothlorien, its life appears to have been sucked away,
judging by the distinct boundary between the scorched plains and the grassland.
Dense underbrush makes passage north into the forest impossible, although entry
to the east is possible. Plains can be found to the south and west.
name: Scorched Plains

desc:
The trees here are smaller and more sparsely placed than their cousins to the
north. The ground is slightly damp due to the wetlands to the west and south; a
damp woody smell hangs in the air. In the distant north a faint glimmer of
mellyrn can be seen, a reminder of the beauty found not so far from here.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The large trees grow sparse here as the forest comes to an end, giving way to
plains to the south and east. Although less impressive than their kind to the
north, these trees are still awe-inspiring, and much larger than the trees of
the other forests. The ground is slightly damp here.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Leaving the cover of the forest, the plains stretch out into the open lands
south of Lothlorien. The ground is somewhat wet, allowing one to see the
footprints of occasional animals distinctly. The vast plains continue to the
east and south. The forest lies to the north and west.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Vigorously growing grass covers the plains. Many rocks are scattered across the
grass bed; some of the rocks are quite big and it is not clear how they got
here. Plains stretch out in all directions, reaching the edge of the forest to
the north. The scent of mellyrn drifts hazily in from the north, drawing the
senses closer to the beauty and awe of Lothlorien.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The grass here is trampled down and matted into a natural pathway. Winding
through the plains, the path leads east and south. The majestic forest of
Lothlorien can be seen to the north. Plains continue in all directions.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
Scattered trees and bushes can be seen here and there, growing larger and closer
to each other in the north and almost disappearing to the south. The grass is
matted down as if many large animals regularly pass this way. One can only
surmise that food or water is plentiful in this region.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
The soft brushwood blends into the low grass, and southwards the vegetation
thins out. Except for the shadowy forest in the north, not much breaks the
horizon here. The vast plains seem lifeless, yet you can make out faint tracks
in the dusty ground, indicating that some small wildlife actually thrives here.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A green carpet of grass prevails here, only challenged by an occasional tree.
The trees are of a low, persistent type with dry, twisting branches and small
dark leaves, adapted to survive in harsh conditions. To the north the edge of
the forest towers, dominating the horizon.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The belt of shrubbery grows sparsely here, and it is not necessary to push
through the bushes. Occasionally small animals can be heard prowling among the
undergrowth through the bush roots, probably looking for food. Northwards the
shrubbery becomes more dense.
name: Sparse Bushes

desc:
Something has burned down a large area of shrubbery here, revealing the bare,
sandy soil. Pitch black, partly burned branches lie everywhere and pieces of
wood litter the ground. What has caused the destruction is not apparent;
possibly the shrubbery has been set on fire by lightning.
name: Burned Plains

desc:
This secluded hollow is well protected from the wind and grows many kinds of
plants. Near the bottom of the hollow lie some square-shaped stony formations
which seem man-made, though very old. They are completely overgrown and covered
in moss, but seem too regular to be natural. Possibly they are remains of a
house which has once stood here, for they look like foundations of a building.
name: Dry Grasslands

desc:
A row of bushes grows here lining the old path which cuts through the hillside,
dropping towards the bottom of a shallow valley to the south. Here the valley
walls are not high, and have probably once been used for cultivation. Now only
grass prevails on them, and the whole settlement of the valley is but a
half-forgotten memory.
name: Bushes by the Path

desc:
Clumps of short bushes dominate the landscape here. Far to the east and west
this wide belt of brushland extends. The bushes are of the kind which thrives in
these dry conditions; the long, tangled branches are protected by sharp thorns.
Only stunted grass and brambles grow amidst the thorny bushes.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
Between the forest in the north and the line of bushes in the south this wide,
open field grows mostly grass and low shrubbery. The ground is even and easy to
travel over. A small path winds its way to the north, picking its way through
the vegetation.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
Lush, green grasses flow smoothly across the land, providing a carpet of
habitation for the creatures of the area. The heady scent of the forest to the
north floats occasionally through the air, drawing one's spirit towards the
trees' leafy canopy.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A sparse belt of shrubbery runs here, following the border of the mighty Golden
Wood which lies to the north. The air here is fresh and the wind carries a
sweet, inviting scent from the depths of the forest. The ground is uneven, as
the wide roots of the bushes spread all around.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The trees lie sparsely here while other vegetation prevails. A dense layer of
green, narrow-leaved bushes grows here. Trodding through the carpet of shrubbery
would leave clear tracks but the brush seems to mould and reform quickly,
leaving little evidence of passage through it.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
Though the trees grow more sparsely here than to the north the underbrush is
thick and soft, almost sinking under your weight. The visibility to the south is
good and although the grasslands are level here, the shapes of hills and valleys
are evident in the distance.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
Among the low, soft carpet of brushwood an occasional tree rises from the green
sea of grass. The trees here on the eaves of the forest seem less healthy than
those in the north; round clumps of fungi grow on them and they are lower and
more twisted. Southwards the trees disappear completely, giving way to the
rolling plains which begin here at the edge of Lorien and continue for miles to
the south.
name: Scattered Trees

desc:
A few trees grow here, their branches dancing merrily in the wind. The trees
appear to bend slightly north, as if they could sense the fresh scent of
Lothlorien and wished to get closer to the eaves of the great wood. Plains can
be seen to the south and west. The sparsely growing trees continue to the east.
name: Sparse Foliage

desc:
The spacious plains open up, leaving a great forest to the north. Small bushes
and lesser trees mark the border of the forest. The flat terrain seems to
stretch endlessly into the southern horizon. The sweet scent of the forest is
still in the air, a gentle reminder of the beauty of the mellyrn of Lothlorien.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The ground here is somewhat wet, the moisture apparently due to the forest that
rises to the north and west. The array of rocks and trees to the north seems
impassable. Quite a few footprints can be seen in the soft ground by the rocks,
leading west into the forest. The footprints appear to have been made by an orc
war party. The plains continue to the east and south.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The mellyrn here are not as numerous as to the north, yet their presence is
quite visible for they are the source of a faint golden radiance that permeates
the air. The forest ends to the south, giving way to vast plains that appear
very fertile. The soft ground shows many footprints left by what might have been
a war party of some sort. The tracks lead north into the forest and disappear
there.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A dense blanket of leaves blocks most of the sky here. Nonetheless, the forest
is very light. The soft ground below the magnificent mellyrn reveals many
footprints of forest dwellers that passed here. The forest continues in all
directions, growing in density to the north. The presence of something watching
this forest is quite obvious, but nothing can be seen; it is more like a
feeling.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The majestic, tall trees seem to keep a close, silent watch on this forest. A
peaceful feeling is in the air, as though no evil could abide in this place for
long. Small bushes can be seen growing beside the trees. The branches of the
bushes are covered with an assortment of wild berries.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Covering the sky above with leafy branches, the trees seem to sway back and
forth with the wind, slowly singing a song without words. A small forest path
can be seen to the north; the trees gently embrace it as it winds through the
forest. More woodlands lie to the east and west. Dense underbrush makes southern
passage impossible.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A small path starts here and runs north through the forest. Branches of pines
and firs overhang the path. The air below them is scented with resin dripping
down from their tips. Alongside the path, below two closely standing trees,
remains of what appears to have been a tree-house lie shattered on the ground.
At first glance the rubble seems to be just a pile of broken wooden planks.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Less magnificent than their counterparts to the north, the trees here are
nonetheless almost breathtaking. The sweet scent of firs and pines fills the
air. To the north, a small clearing in the forest can be seen, beyond which
rises a stand of mellyrn. Plains lie to the south and west, while the forest
continues to the north and east.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A light grass dusts the ground of the plains here, slightly damp from the
increased moisture in the area where the plains gradually turn into forest. To
the east and north large trees can be seen rising above the landscape,
majestically spanning the sky. The plains continue to the south, where the
ground starts to become firmer.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Wavy grass covers much of the ground, interspersed by a few small shrubs. At
some distance to the west and to the north the beginnings of a large forest can
be seen. A few scrappy trees struggle in the poor soil along the southern border
of the forest. Rolling softly, the plains gently rise and fall to the south and
southeast for as far as one can see.
name: Plains near a Forest

desc:
Very little light filters through the trees here, allowing only parts of the
forest around this flet to show themselves depending on the angle of the sun.
The forest floor is not visible from here, making it seem miles away. The
mallorn-trees seem to glow faintly, emitting a sweet fragrance. The forest to
the south is thin enough that this tree offers a good view of lands in that
direction, and this flet could be used as an outpost.
name: On a Flet

desc:
Branches reach out towards the walls of the broken-down mill, providing a
sheltered area which apparently serves as an animal den. The humidity of this
place most likely made it very habitable, once the mill had fallen into disarray
and those taking care of it had left.
name: Inside the Den

desc:
The basement of the old water mill is damp and musty, even though no water has
been pumped here for a very long time. The equipment and inner workings of the
mill are rusted and corroded; most of it has begun to fall off and decay.
Without water it is likely that no need was found to repair it. A large pile of
branches is resting against the northern wall.
name: Mill Basement

desc:
The entrance to this old water mill is very dusty, as though nary a person has
been here in many years. Without water flowing to the mill it seems to have been
abandoned by even the local wildlife, as there is no longer anything here for
them. Stairs lead up and down. Some debris can be seen in a corner, possibly the
result of a battle once fought here.
name: Ruined Mill

desc:
From the top of the old water mill the surrounding land can be seen in all
directions. The now-dry streambed to the east, that used to provide the valley
with water, can be seen winding its way past the mill. A flight of stairs heads
back down into the mill.
name: Top of the Ruined Mill

desc:
This old cellar looks as if it had been used for food storage a long time ago.
The walls, the floor and the ceiling still appear to be in good shape, even
though the air is moist and drops of water condense on every smooth surface.
Judging by the trash and the remains of a small fire on the stone floor, this
place was used by travellers that stayed here for a night or more. A large
arched door is in the southern wall. Slightly incongruous in these somewhat
plain surroundings, the door is very carefully crafted and appears untouched by
time.
name: The Cellar

desc:
This is a small room with broken shelves along one wall and a large ruined
closet on the other wall. The room is softly lit by small glowing crystal prisms
hanging from a thin silver chain on the ceiling. In the northern wall is a
magnificent arched door decorated with fine carvings. As the room does not
appear to be inhabited, the lack of dust on the floor is intriguing.
name: A Dimly-lit Chamber

desc:
This is a rather large room with a large table and some ruined chairs in one
corner and the remains of a couch and a smaller table in the other. The walls
are decorated with paintings, a few portraits and one landscape. A dark curtain
covers the western wall. The lack of dust is intriguing, but even more so are
the small glowing crystal prisms that hang from a thin silver chain in the
middle of the room, bathing the place in a soft cozy light.
name: A Dimly-lit Hall

desc:
This appears to be an old study room. The walls are lined with shelves. Several
broken chests lie on the floor and a large desk stands by one wall. The interior
of the room has not survived the aging well, but the structure itself looks as
if it could last for many decades to come. The eastern wall is covered by a
large dark curtain which oddly enough looks untouched by time.
name: The Study Chamber

desc:
This small cave is dug out of the soft, fertile soil; the digging appears to
have been done by a large animal. The floor and the walls are of caked mud, and
a muddy hole leads back up to the surface. To the east, a dark recess can be
seen. A sharp, musty smell pervades the air.
name: Muddy Entrance

desc:
This room, like the entrance, shows signs of having been dug by a large animal
with sharp claws. The room is damp, perhaps absorbing moisture from an
underground water source. The floor consists of soil and mud and has been packed
down hard by the constant trampling of whatever creature lives here.
name: Damp Refuge

desc:
This dark and damp alcove is filled with musty, foul-smelling rubbish. The earth
walls are almost covered by the running water. The floor is covered by one foot
of cold and dirty water. The only exit is an opening to the north which seems to
lead to a larger cave of some kind.
name: Muddy Cave

desc:
This cave is large enough to hold a small army and appears to be the centre of
this underground complex. Three smaller caves are connected to it. The uneven
walls are of earth and clay. Water runs down the walls, and about one foot of
water covers the floor. A strong, stifling smell of mould comes from the north
and south.
name: Muddy Cave

desc:
This alcove seems to be a sleeping chamber of some kind. A large rock nest can
be seen in the back. Water runs down the walls, and the floor is covered by one
foot of cold and dirty water. The air is almost unbreathable, filled with the
heavy odour of mould that seems to come from the nest and the walls of the cave.
The only exit is to the south, to what appears to be a larger cave.
name: Muddy Cave

desc:
The cave is dark and moist. The walls are hard-packed earth. Water runs down the
walls, and the earth floor is covered by one foot of cold, dirty water. The air
is heavy, damp and musty. The musty odour seems to come from an opening to the
east which appears to be the only exit from this dark cave. This should
encourage you to head that way, but there is something scary about this place.
name: Muddy Cave

desc:
Although the foliage of the tree is dense and thick the gaps between the
branches provide a good view of the surrounding area. A vast forest can be seen
far away to the north; a couple of small hillocks dot the level terrain to the
east, west and south. The branches appear to fully support your weight and it is
unlikely any of them will break.
name: Top of the Tree

desc:
Dug out of the inside of the hill, this bear den seems cozy enough. A small pool
of dirty water can be seen on the ground near the back of the den. The water is
apparently from an underground source and appears clean enough to drink. The
earthen floor is quite smooth and clean, which might indicate present or recent
habitation of the den.
name: Inside the Den

desc:
Flowing gently eastward, the deep waters of the Celebrant flow strong. Clear and
cold, the water has travelled all the way from the heights of the Misty
Mountains, restlessly searching for the great rivers of the east. Banking the
river are the wooded slopes of the enchanted forest of Lothlorien, standing
forever watchful and guarding over the river's passage through the Golden Wood.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Flowing gently, but with hidden strength, the Celebrant glitters and sparkles as
small eddies churn and ripple across the surface. The banks of the river are
covered by tall mellyrn that stand in lines like an honour guard, showing its
respect to a passing king.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Bending sharply eastwards, the river flows steadily onwards on its eternal quest
to join the river Anduin far far to the east. The deep and clear water is
chilling to the bone, as it has travelled far from the peaks of the Misty
Mountains before passing beneath the eaves of the Golden Wood. Lining the banks,
the proud and mighty trees of Lothlorien stand tall and solemn, almost as if
watching over the river's passage, making sure it flows unhindered towards its
goal.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Running in the deep ravine that it has carved out for itself throughout the
ages, the Celebrant lies shadowed by the wooded banks that border it. Swiftly
flowing, the cold and clear water makes its way steadily onwards to the east and
the great river Anduin.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Rushing swiftly through the widening ravine, the Celebrant turns sharply
northwards. Even though forced to change its course, it seems anxious to reach
its faraway goal. The deep and cold river passes below the wooded river banks
that are lined with towering mellyrn that stand guard, forever watching and
guiding the loved river onward.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Flowing slowly eastwards, the cold waters of the Celebrant continue their long
journey begun high up in the Misty Mountains and strive towards the east to meet
the great river Anduin. The clear water runs deep and slow here, and the sloping
banks are lined by the eternally watching mellyrn.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The deep, cold and clear Celebrant flows steadily towards its goal far away in
the east, the great river Anduin. The steep banks of the river are lined by the
towering mellyrn, standing solemn and silent as guardians, forever watching the
water flow by.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Slowly flowing onwards, the deep river gently twists and turns as the wooded
river banks move farther apart. Lining the banks are the towering mellyrn,
standing like guardians as the cold and clear water runs without hesitation
towards the great Anduin far away to the east.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Hurling itself northwards, the deep and clear water rushes between the rocky
river banks, its wild pace gradually slowing as the river widens. The rushing
water sends echoes reverberating throughout the forested river banks, almost
drowning out the soft whispers of the breeze that gently rocks the surrounding
trees.
name: Celebrant

desc:
In the narrow ravine, small whirlpools dance across the turbulent surface,
eddying and sucking at everything. Water smashes into the rocky walls and hurls
itself onto the jagged rocks rising in the middle of the raging river. The roar
of the water drowns out all other sounds and echoes sharply out towards the
solemn forest that stands above.
name: Turbulent Waters

desc:
The river bends from the west to the south, its swift clear waters continuing
their course ever eastward, toward the fair elven city that lies within the
Naith. Only the true of heart may attempt to cross into the Naith, and as one
peers into the forest ahead, it is not even possible to fathom how many eyes are
watching.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The Celebrant makes a bend from the north to the east. The clear waters make it
possible to catch an occasional glimpse of the fish that swim just below the
surface of this life-giving river. These fish help feed the elven inhabitants of
the Golden Wood, while the waters sustain a myriad of plant life.
name: River Bend

desc:
The river takes a sharp southward turn here, leaving small whirlpools in the
recesses of the northern bank. Small golden flowers washed away from the shore
are captured in those eddies and dance rapidly in circles upon the shimmering
silver carpet of Celebrant before getting caught up in the current and
disappearing downstream. The mallorn-trees of gold and silver grow on the banks
of Silverlode. A tiny trail is hugging it very closely from the north.
name: A Bend in the River

desc:
The Silverlode bends east here, guarding the southern border of the Gore from
the tumults of the world beyond. Elves call it the Naith of Lorien - the land
that lies like a spearhead between the arms of Celebrant and Anduin the Great.
It is well protected indeed, for evil is loth to cross the shining cold waters
of the stream which has been touched by the mysterious powers of the Lady of the
Golden Wood.
name: A Bend in the River

desc:
Swift and strong runs the river here. Clean and deep are its waters. Many small
stones are scattered over its bottom carried down here from the Misty Mountains
and then away toward Anduin. A great golden forest rises on its banks, its trees
standing above the water like timeless guardians of the First Age protecting the
last remnant of the Elder Days left on Middle-earth.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The cold waters of Celebrant dance and ripple with many colours as it makes its
way east. Mighty golden trees line the banks, their boughs gently swaying in the
wind. The forest is sparse to the south, but it thickens north as if trying to
conceal the mysteries that lie within the Naith of Lorien, perhaps the most
perilous and beautiful place in the waking world.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Tall trees with golden crowns reflect in the river. Silverlode they call it in
the far lands, perhaps for the silvery trunks of the trees that reflect in its
water giving it an elusive quality. A small trail runs in and out of the forest
along the north bank. Watchfulness permeates the wood on either side of the
river, for no evil can cross it undetected and walk the Naith of Lorien
unpunished.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Entering a narrow passage in the ravine, the river hurls itself forward at an
incredible pace, smashing into the rocky walls and sending out a deafening roar
accompanied by a spray of white foam. Like sentinels, the grey-clad mellyrn
stand above, silently observing your passage.
name: Rapid Celebrant

desc:
As the wooded river banks close in, the languid flow of the Celebrant quickens,
hurrying eastward. The voice of the water pouring over the stones is sent
splashing and babbling into the sparse stands of mellyrn on either side. On the
northern bank a small trail follows the river, darting in and out of sight as it
winds amongst the great grey boles of the trees.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Celebrant becomes narrower here, turning fast, deep, and furious. The banks
stand high on either side and fall off sharply providing little foothold.
Mallorn trees, Lorien's faithful guardians, shield the riverbanks in a thick
endless fence, beckoning one to continue east as the stream flows, rather then
risk the forest fortress of the magic powers that hold this land.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The river falls down a shallow stair, which is not tall enough to cause a
splash. A thin layer of mist gathers above the water downstream compounding the
mystique of the forest of tall silvery trees that grow along the banks. The
trees grow as far as the eye can see, upriver and downriver, on both banks along
the stream that swiftly appears from the west and, just as rapidly, vanishes in
the east.
name: Down the Shallow Stair

desc:
Silverlode journeys across the land from the springs of Dimrill Dale. Joined by
many smaller streams, it becomes a swift and powerful river. Its surface,
playfully uneven, flickers with distorted reflections of the mallorn trees that
flank its sides. Its waters are clear, and small fish can be seen darting here
and there near the banks.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Transitory, yet magnificent and calming, the waters of Celebrant speed to their
ultimate destination. Irrevocably, they rush in a shower of falling stars, never
to see this place again, yet still a part of the whole. They are droplets of
water that may have heard Ulmo speak at the dawn of the First Age, or have been
part of the treacherous currents that swallowed Numenor of the damned when the
world was transformed. No one will know, for they will hold their peace forever.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The river is wide and open here. Tall mellyrn that flank its banks raise their
arms toward the stars and do not intrude upon the river. The sky of Lorien is
reflected in the water, on clear days transforming the middle of the river into
an interplay of blues and whites instead of Celebrant's customary silver.
name: Celebrant

desc:
The river flows east here. The grey and gold forest on both sides is interrupted
north by a small depressed clearing, barely visible from the river but for two
square pillars of white stone. An image of a star is carved into the misaligned
top of the east most column. The guiding rune seems to shimmer in the shadow of
the trees, its light a memory of comfort to the weary, a glimmer of hope for the
dispirited.
name: Celebrant

desc:
Here a huge branch has fallen, due to a recent storm as it appears. It is
possible to get around the branch by squeezing close to the great trees that
rise all around. The fallen branch has opened up a patch of the forest sky, and
already hordes of vines, brush, and saplings have grown up and around the
branch.
name: Downed Branch in the Forest

desc:
A faint trail meanders through the thick undergrowth here, getting more distinct
