to the east and almost disappearing among the trees to the west. To the south,
the Silverlode cuts a deep channel through the forest. It is possible to
scramble down the riverbank to the water. Beyond the river the unprotected
forest lies, different in look and feel. The short distance between the northern
and southern bank feels like a great gap, as by crossing it one would leave the
Naith of Lorien, the protected elven land.
name: At the Border of the Naith

desc:
A pleasant trail winds its way right along the edge of the river. It swerves up
and down, left and right, as it follows the carefree dance of Celebrant on its
way south. On its other side, the trail is bordered by an imposing forest of
mellyrn, same as can be seen on the other bank of the river. Small pebbles are
scattered along the path. Judging from the intermittent patches of grass on the
south bank, the river used to be farther from the path once before claiming its
current bed.
name: Trail beside Celebrant

desc:
A narrow clearing, a soft carpet of green grass and golden elanor, is squeezed
in between the golden forest and the silver stream. A tiny trail runs southeast
here, away from the horrors of Moria and toward the wonders of the Heart of
Lorien. The clearing slopes gently toward the river, whose waters whoosh as an
occasional wave washes a golden flower away in its embrace.
name: Along the Borders of the Naith

desc:
A clear and pleasant trail winds its way along Celebrant. It bends from west to
south here following the course of the river along its northern bank. The trail
seems barely trodden for it was made by the light elven feet of Galadhrim
rangers who go across the river to gather news and to defend the borders of
Lothlorien from the evil that walks Dimrill Dale and the Mountains beyond.
name: Trail through the Golden Wood

desc:
A smooth narrow trail runs under a great archway of mallorn trees here. Their
golden boughs interweave forming an intricate pattern of shimmering yellow on
blue above the head of a rare traveller. Celebrant glitters through the trees to
the southwest. Its melodious soft voice can be heard for many miles around. The
trail bends from north to east here as it follows the river on its way to the
sea, as the spirit of Nimrodel rushes to be reunited with that of Amroth, her
lover.
name: Under the Eaves of the Golden Wood

desc:
Smooth and straight runs the trail here. The smell of trodden grass and that of
the river to the south combine to become, by a peculiar stroke of fate, that of
seawater. The voice of Celebrant magnifies this illusion, for here it sounds
like a thousand waves beating tirelessly at the forgotten shore of a land that
is no more.
name: Trail by the River

desc:
A large grove of golden mellyrn presides over the forest here. Their great grey
trunks are of mighty girth and gigantic height. A bright vertical streak fades
in and out of existence upon the bark of one of the trees as a light wind
disturbs the forest. Patches of tiny golden flowers grow beneath the closely
growing trees and scintillate merrily when they catch light. The river whispers
gently and peacefully to the southwest.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Eternal twilight reigns here, for the crowns of mellyrn are too thick and well
spread to let sun in during the day or starlight during the night. Yet the air
shimmers with an eerie afterglow that plays upon the silver bark of the trees
and makes dust particles in the air shine. Pale niphredil grows amid the short
grass and the snowflakes of silvery moss.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The long, delicate but insistent fingers of ivy-like underbrush caress the
flawless silvery bark of the mallorn trees. It grows to the height of human
waist here, impeding passage of anything live. Celebrant's calming song comes
from the southwest, but it is barely loud enough to be heard here. Its timbre
varies with the wind as if the forest and the river were singing a duet.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The golden forest thickens to the south and thins out to the east of here. The
solid grey boles of the older mallorn trees stand firm and tall, witnesses to
centuries of peace that came before. The slender stems of younger mellyrn quiver
with the wind while maintaining the air of antiquity, which weighs on the forest
like the drops of water weigh on the leaves after a summer rain.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The Golden Wood surrounds you in all its majesty and magnificence. The silvery
branches of mellyrn and their golden crowns are similar to a stained window with
pieces of sky strewn in among silver and gold. The picture keeps changing with
every wisp of the wind. Celebrant's subdued voice comes from the south
interwoven with the rustling of trees and the singing of birds.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The mallorn trees wind in a circle around a small glade. When light of the skies
falls onto the glade, it catches in the golden tree-crowns and makes the
majestic silvery trunks gleam with silvery radiance. The lush green grass is
steeped with moisture, being so close to the river. All in all, this place is a
scene of the delicate beauty that can be found only in the Golden Wood.
name: Golden Circle

desc:
The forest here is a tonal pandemonium in shades of pearl, silver, and grey,
with hints of mauve and blue peeking out of the shadows. Vermillion of the
mallorn crowns accentuates the vertiginous nature of the colour scheme native to
this land. For indeed, the dazzling visual display, that is this forest, is like
no other spot upon the mortal lands.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The stylish caprice, that is the green strands of ivy laying its way through the
gossamer-soft silvery moss, is glimmering within the ever-moving gold-tinted
patches of light. It filters through the gilded branches of mellyrn growing
here. A quiet forceful purpose is present in the flamboyant tangle that produces
this effervescence, though there is no self-evident recurrent motif.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Droplets of light shimmer into existence and vanish just as quickly as the wind
touches the golden crowns of the mellyrn, casting shadows on their stiffly
upright naked grey boles. No underbrush grows here but the silvery moss that
requires little light to spread. A peaceful atmosphere stretches for miles
around, for few things are capable of disturbing the magics of this ancient
wood.
name: Deep in the Golden Wood

desc:
Silver trunks and golden leaves or flowers - it is a simple yet utterly
breathtaking combination. Such is the forest in this part of Lorien where only
mallorn trees grow. The underbrush is thick here, but soft and supple to the
touch, like birch sprouts in spring. Celebrant sings its lullaby to the south,
but whether it is for the gentle creatures of the Naith or for the few
travellers and elven rangers away from home, one cannot tell.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
This is a snug little corner of this sprawling forest. The light does not
penetrate too deep in here, allowing thick mosses and little ferns to flourish
in this part of the wood. Tall, thick-trunked mellyrn block the way to the west,
north and east, but to the south the forest opens before you.
name: Secluded Corner of the Wood

desc:
The trail snakes in and out of the golden forest running all the while along the
north bank of the river. The south bank of the Silverlode is shrouded in grey
mist which makes it seem far more remote than it actually is. Tall mellyrn are
stretching their golden boughs over the trail as if trying to shelter it from
unfriendly eyes lurking in the haze to the south, but on the land of Lorien no
shadows lie.
name: The Forest Trail

desc:
The trail dives into a grassy hollow, which is concealed by the overhanging
branches of mellyrn from all sides except south, where Celebrant flows
unhindered. Two short square pillars of smooth white stone guard the clearing
close to the water. The top of the eastern column is cut off diagonally so that
when looking from the river, the guiding star carved into its top can be seen.
The western pillar has a metal ring attached to it, and the floor of the hollow
close to it is lined with traces of boats dragged ashore. The noses of a few
boats are peeking from the grass.
name: Across the Grassy Hollow

desc:
The Elven trail runs through the golden forest, unnoticeable but for the lighter
bough dance and the lack of underbrush activity. The magnificent mallorn-trees,
the only trees in this forest, are crowned with gold and garbed in grey with a
silvery sheen on the southern parts of their boles, the latter being just a
reflection of the swift waters of Celebrant.
name: The Forest Trail

desc:
A trodden path goes up and down a green hillock here. Just to the south,
Celebrant glistens silver-grey behind a row of mallorn trees. The hillock rises
as an island amidst the sea of gold, its mellyrn threatening to hide the sun
with their sprawling silvery branches.
name: The Forest Trail

desc:
The elven path widens here to form a small circular clearing, encased, like a
precious jewel in a necklace, within a ring of stunningly beautiful leafless
mellyrn. A round flat stone, that travellers can rest their weary feet on, is
set in the middle of the clearing. The trail runs around the stone eastward
toward the heart of the wood and the final elven abode.
name: A Stone upon the Forest Path

desc:
Eastward bound, the path goes downhill here, briefly disappearing into the thick
underbrush at the bottom of the hill. It runs under the eaves of the mellyrn of
the legendary Golden Wood, the subject of many a tale in the world outside, both
good and evil. Their grey stalks enclose the path, and their wide spread
branches with golden leaves or flowers upon them shelter it from the unfriendly
eyes.
name: The Forest Path

desc:
A lightly trodden path winds its way through the Golden Wood. The forest is
thick here. Its low hanging branches are obscuring the view and impeding the
way. Silver-wrapped Celebrant glistens through the golden filigree of mallorn
crowns to the south. The trail, adorned with ghostly niphredil, runs west to
east here toward the heart of Lorien.
name: The Forest Path

desc:
Running east and west, the small forest trail follows the Celebrant, balancing
precariously on its steep bank. Standing tall and proud, though not so numerous,
the golden trees of Lothlorien, the mellyrn, are lined up like an army column
along the trail. Soft whispers can be heard from within the ranks of the trees
as the wind gently brushes past the great boughs.
name: A Forest Trail

desc:
Running atop the sheer cliff, this small trail follows the curve of the fair
Celebrant like a faithful lover, never straying from its side. The drop to the
river is a fair distance and the raging waters speak of a lost love too sad to
endure. Just north of the trail, the sparse forest stands serene and
contemplating, whispering softly the concealed words of passion and comfort.
name: Cliff above the Celebrant

desc:
Rising high above the Celebrant, the rocky outcrop lies bare as all the soil has
been swept away by gentle winds. Far below, the white rapids rage unhindered,
casting white foam-spray high into the air and creating a deafening sound. Not
far off, to your northwest, grow the thick mallorn woods, striving towards the
distant sky, ever reaching higher and higher.
name: Cliff above the Celebrant

desc:
High above the Celebrant, close to the edge of the cliff runs this small trail.
Bordered by the dense mallorn forest and the deep ravine in which the river
flows, it offers a splendid view. The soothing sound of the flowing water from
below blends with the whispering of the forest as the wind gently blows through
the tree-tops.
name: Cliff above the Celebrant

desc:
The forest here is sparse, allowing the tall grey mellyrn to stretch their
boughs towards the skies above. The spacing of the trees allows the light of sun
and stars to reach the ground, dispelling the grey shadows that rest between the
great silvery boles of the noble trees.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
Just north of the small forest trail, the woods become less dense, allowing the
light of sun, moon or stars to filter down and illuminate the trunks, creating
marvellous displays of shadow and light. As the wind blows, the patterns twist
and change, giving the impression of movement among the trees.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
Specks of gold fly across one's field of vision, as the mallorn leaves succumb
to the light wind that seemingly chases the pale ghosts of antiquity down the
untrodden pathways of time. The age-old growth impedes passage although light is
ample here and the mallorn trees are sparse.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
A complicated sheer arrangement of fine white branches, silhouetteted against
the sky, completes this forest sanctuary. The effervescent golden crowns of the
mellyrn project a gossamer web of ghostly light on the ground, highlighting the
patches of grass dotted with diaphanous white flowers of niphredil. The branches
grow lower and thicker in the southern part of the forest, impeding the passage,
but offering even more compelling views into the nature's stained glass windows
to the world.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The mallorn trees of this grove are quite typical of their kind, but for the
naked limbs raised to the sky. The forest here has the untanned look of late
winter for the trees have neither the golden flowers of early spring, nor the
gilded leaves of late autumn that bestow upon the mallorn crowns their customary
burnished royal look. The loss lends the forest an eerily pale outline and a
subtle air of vulnerability. Unyielding but lush, some evergreen bushes linger
further along south, toward the warmer parts of the land.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The tiny sparks of gold and silver reverberate along the shimmering expanse of
the boundless grass blanket that conceals the ground. This arresting effect
comes from the fragile stalks and delicate flowers of the ghostlike niphredil
and the thin semi-transparent mallorn leaves, somewhat disturbed by a faint
wind. The mallorn trees reign the elven forest as far as the eye can see, though
they seem to be particularly vigilant and implacable further south.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The mallorn trees stretch languidly toward the sky, dazzling in their display of
the luscious golden crowns and the glorious pallor of the boles. The young
offshoots, shorter and scantier clad, are languishing in the shade of their
mighty parents, content to wait in twilight for the dawning of a new age.
Abundant green underbrush camouflages the ground and partly the view to the
south.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Grand, tall, and erect, the mallorn trees of Lorien are a marvel to behold. They
are a solid presence, a promise of permanence and peace in the world where
barriers crumble and races dwindle and disappear. At the same time, they seem
eerily distant, for such wholesomeness and purity of shape and colour does not
exist within the Middle-earth anymore, but for a few patches of elven land that
have mysterious powers of their own. To the south, the trees huddle
protectively, spaced much closer than in other places. But while one's passage
might be inconvenienced by this, the view remains just as commanding.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
This part of the wood seems younger than the rest, if one can compare eternity
with eternity that is a few ages less. The golden mallorn trees are slender and
tall here, their bodies shooting into the sky like arrows, but their crowns not
far spread enough yet to hide the sky. Gold, silver and green dominate the wood
- the colours that are pure and timeless as the land of Lorien itself. These
colours are significantly deeper to the south where the forest thickens like a
protective barrier.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The ancient forest soars silently about you as if transplanted here from some
long-forgotten lay. Its silvery trees, tall and mighty, are crowned with a roof
of golden fire. Light fresh wind plays joyfully among their silvery trunks. At a
distance, a river murmur can be heard from what seems like every direction - a
trick of sound for the forest of gold and green stretches for miles blocking
every view.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The silver mellyrn part here, the thinning wood giving way to a grassy clearing,
its floor studded with tiny star-shaped golden flowers. The ground rises to the
east where the clearing turns into a hill upon which a large grey monolith can
be seen. The golden forest stretches for miles and miles around you. The raised
clearing gives you a good view of its golden roof which staunchly hides
everything that goes on underneath it.
name: A Sloping Clearing

desc:
North and downhill, the ground plunges from a high clearing and into an ever-
thickening golden canopy that is Lorien's indigenous flora. The mallorn trees
that constitute it stand out of it, drawn in precise and sure strokes, but with
a mysterious suggestion echoing in the aristocratic curves of their branches.
name: Into the Forest

desc:
The path weaves south to north here, making its way through sage bushes. The
bushes are as high as human waist, though it is nearly impossible to tell one
bush from the next, for their small but splayed green leaves, whose backs are
tinted silver, make the surroundings look like one grey chaotic spider web laid
over a field of green. Sweet herbal smell rises above the path from the sage
leaves crushed under the feet of careless travellers.
name: Path through Sage Brush

desc:
The golden trees are edging their way up the hill. A tall grey monolith tapering
toward the top but with a square base dominates the hilltop. A single rune, an
elegant and elaborate "G", is carved into its sunset side. Its maker used no
ordinary chisel and hammer, for the rune glimmers faintly like a rising star as
if containing some fiery power. Its energy is seemingly similar to that of the
land around it, yet subtly different, the way two stars in the evening sky are
different and yet the same. Twelve round marked stones lie in a straight line to
the east of the monolith, and their twelve siblings line up to the west, the
shadow of the runed stone in the middle indicating time of the day.
name: The Hill of a Thousand Stars

desc:
The forest of tall grey trees with golden crowns as is typical of this land
grows here. Their silver-skinned boles lift their gold studded arms to the sky.
At the feet of the trees, upon the grass of cheerful green, elanor grows. Its
golden stars shimmer upon the glossy green carpet as their real counterparts do
in the sky on a clear moonless night. The ground rises to the north as the trees
gradually give way to a small clearing.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Past, future, and present manifest themselves in the unearthly silver and gold
of the mallorn trees, which in their royal bearing and majesty give no
indication that there ever was or will be time when they were or will be
anything other than what they are now: the living guardians of the last truly
elven land with proud grey trunks and fiery crowns that put all the dwarven
treasures to shame.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The bare mallorn trunks, their soft bark gleaming damp silver in the dimness of
the ancient wood, stretch their arms leisurely toward each other. The great
boughs form a delicately wrought mosaic - golden against the blue, grey or black
of the sky. A pale square of a flet hangs high up among the branches, though
there seems to be no obvious foot or hand support that would allow to reach it.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The sparsely growing mallorn trees give way to a cacophony of colours: the green
grass here is studded with flowers in an unimaginable number of different shades
of gold, yellow, and orange. The colour of elanor changes subtly as it moves
from the cover of the great silver trees toward the unshaded spots among them.
The unseen birds sing merrily from the branches of the mallorn trees adding a
welcome touch to the festive mood of the forest.
name: The Light Wood

desc:
The floor of the wood is golden here, whether from fallen leaves or the flowers
of elanor, and golden is the roof, and its pillars are of silver, for the bark
of the trees is smooth and grey. Shadows fall and waver in the breeze that
whispers of times long gone and secrets lost, but the land of Lorien stays
peaceful and unscathed against the tide of time.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The dim shadows thrown by the mighty mallorn trees play in the grass forming the
ever-shifting patterns of light and dark at their feet. Now and again, a silver
star-shaped flower catches light, and then fades again into a short blink of
obscurity. The silver-grey forest stretches out in all directions as far as the
eye can see.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
As the tall mellyrn stretch their boughs towards the open sky, the grounds below
are cast into shadow. The wind plays amongst the branches, shifting the shadows
into forms that dance amongst the great silvery trunks to the soft whispering of
the proud trees.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
The great trees of the Golden Wood stand tall here, rising high into the sky.
Yet even so, they grow sparse enough to allow some light to fall upon the
landscape below, creating a fierce contrast of gold and shadow. The forest grows
more dense and solemn to the north, whereas to the south it opens up, becoming
lush and bright. You can barely make out a small forest path running at the edge
of the forest to the east of you. From beyond it you can hear the Celebrant
rushing forth eagerly along its stone bed. Here within the woods the faint
whispers of the trees and the song of several hidden birds fall lightly upon
your ears.
name: Forest at the Trail

desc:
Running high above the Celebrant, this small trail curves gently to follow the
edge of the cliff. Bordering the trail are the grey boles of the Golden Wood,
the ageless mellyrn standing high and proud as a reminder of the magic that is
the soul of the forest. Softly the trees whisper, while drifting up from below
comes the chittering of the water over the stones.
name: A Bend in the Trail

desc:
The spacious forest is light and open, allowing the light to illuminate the
ground freely, dispelling most shadows. Although the trees rise high, they stand
far apart, with enough space between their lofty crowns to allow watching the
open skies. The wind blows freely here, the soft gusts of cool air gently
rocking the tall trees and creating faint whispers throughout the woods.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
The trees grow more dense here, barring all passage save to those on foot, and
allowing almost no light to penetrate the thick canopy above. Completely
blocking out the sky, the great grey mellyrn form a green vault in which shadows
veil the details of the terrain. To your south, the forest appears to open up,
while to the east and north you can barely make out a faint path tracing its way
through the heart of the golden woods.
name: Golden Woods

desc:
White light shimmers though the taut elegant forms of the mallorn trees like
through a thin veil, accentuated by the golden raiment of their branches. The
clear voices of birds in the sky and the rustle of the wind in the trees enliven
the scene that stretches unchanging as far as the eye can see.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Shadowy spires rise from the ground and disappear into the sky. They are mellyrn
- the ancient watchers of Lorien, and the most obvious testament to the power
that holds this elven land intact despite the ravages and wars around it. The
ground rises some to the south and the forest recedes in that direction.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The sheer moonlight-filled flowers of niphredil carpet the ground among the
trunks of the great mallorn trees. They peek from every nook and corner among
the trees' mighty roots. The dark green sinewy vines of ivy wind around the
lower parts of the mellyrn all the way to the golden wreaths of their lower
branches that creak in unison with the softly sighing wind.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The middle of the round green glade is raised, forming a pedestal for the two
formidable mallorn trees. So much alike they are and so close do they grow, as
if they may well be twins - the two ancient stewards of the never changing wood,
bowing slightly in the opposite directions, watching east and west intently for
the signs that only they will know.
name: In the Shadow of the Twin Mellyrn

desc:
The golden forest is sparse here, for the ground is mostly stone with patches of
typical Lorien light coloured soil appearing here and there. The stone forming
the ground is white and smooth, covered with silvery star-shaped moss in places.
The moss cannot conceal the half-faded inscriptions that cover the stone in
Elven runes. The forest becomes thinner to the south as it follows a downward
slope.
name: The Light Wood

desc:
The golden forest parts here, baring the rocky ground that drops straight down
to the east. A clear spring bubbles out evenly from among the white boulders.
The water runs among the rocks and pours over the cliffside down into the pool
below. A rare mallorn tree grapples the rocks with its roots in search of the
ground to feed from. The view from the cliff east encompasses the eastern Lorien
with fiery mellyrn crowns spanning the leagues till the wispy snake of Anduin.
The river cuts off the forest abruptly and the land beyond becomes grey, barren,
and seemingly uninhabited.
name: Above the Waterfall

desc:
A wall of white stone several spans high blocks passage west. Water pours over
the side of the cliff and plunges down the wall in a single sheet of shimmering
white, collecting in a semicircular pool at its foot. The cliff face descends
rapidly on either side of the wall, forming a wider semicircle from which a path
departs east. The cliffside behind the humming waterfall looks silky smooth, but
for the odd artefact in the middle which glows iridescently.
name: The Wall of Twilight

desc:
The mallorn forest surrounds this wide rectangular platform of white stone from
all sides except to the south. There, it turns out to be the top face of a tall
rock forming the northern side of a semicircular stone well that houses a
shimmering waterfall and a pool of clear water at its foot. A narrow stone
staircase, incised in the wall, descends from this small plateau into the well,
snaking eastwards along the wall as it goes down where the rock surface slopes
more gently.
name: The White Stair

desc:
The silvery mellyrn part here for a forest path studded on either side with
small grey stones. Unseen, the wind sneaks among the mallorn crowns of royal
gold, disturbing them ever so slightly, though not enough to conceal the sky
above the path. The trees are firm and slender - they do not look old, yet they
have a timeless quality about them as if there has never been any change in this
small corner of Middle-earth.
name: A Path in the Golden Wood

desc:
Here amidst the dense grey mellyrn, the great age and wisdom contained within
the Golden Wood seem much more vivid and present, almost tangible. The deep
shadows that lie beneath the boughs seem alive, yet not hostile as small birds
dart in and out among the branches, singing happily.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The proud forest stands towering here, reaching high towards the open skies that
you can discern through the tree-tops. Lush grey and golden mellyrn strive in
friendly competition, growing taller than in most other parts of the Golden
Wood. The open and spacious forest is light and joyous, allowing the sun to
dispel most of the shadows in daytime.
name: Golden Woods

desc:
A dense canopy of green and gold prevents any light from reaching the forest
floor here, forever shrouding the tall silvery tree-boles in shadow. Although
dense and shadowy, the forest is filled with movement and the songs of birds
echoing throughout the vast Golden Wood.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Standing tall and dense, the mellyrn here bar all light from reaching the ground
and create deep shadows that play amongst the foliage and smooth, grey trunks.
Shapes of varying sizes can be seen moving in the distance, ranging from smaller
birds to an occasional stag. As the wind blows through the boughs above, the
Golden Wood whispers softly to you.
name: Dense Golden Forest

desc:
The forest path coming from the north banks sharply down and right around a
green tree-covered hillock. It disappears briefly under the shadow of a large
mallorn tree, located just inside the bend at the foot of the knoll, only to
reappear shortly to the west. The sound of water, the music of wind and
droplets, is lulling travellers in that direction.
name: A Bend in the Forest Path

desc:
The path deliberately winds its way among the mellyrn, slowly travelling beside
the small forest stream barely visible within the woodland just north and east
of you. From within the deep reaches of the enchanted wood you can just make out
the soft song of hidden birds and the gentle whisper of the great grey trees as
the wind gently rocks the ancient boughs, creating faint ripples of sound that
blend with the babbling of the glittering forest stream.
name: A Path through the Forest

desc:
Wedged in between the two trails, this small appendix of the forest stands dense
and proud, towering high into the sky. The great mellyrn sway softly to a gentle
breeze and whisper quietly as the wind sifts through the boughs. Soft singing
from the birds can be heard from within the canopy.
name: Arm of the Forest

desc:
Running east and west, this small trail forms the border between the Golden Wood
and the Celebrant. High above the rushing water, you can still feel the moisture
and hear the joyful song of the flowing river. Blending with the babbling of the
deep Celebrant are the soft whispers of the tall mellyrn standing densely to the
north of the trail.
name: A Forest Trail

desc:
Running along the edge of the slope, this small forest trail is the border
between the river and the forest. West, the mellyrn stand tall and dense,
shadowing the ground, while to the east the silver band of the Celebrant runs
sparkling and dazzling below you. The soft whisper of the forest blends with the
roaring of the fast-flowing water as it hastens towards its companion in the
east.
name: Trail above the Celebrant

desc:
Stretching out towards the great river, the golden forest stand dense in the
wedge between trails. Faintly the song of the Celebrant can be heard from the
east, carrying through the woods. Blending with the music of water, warbling of
birds can be heard accompanying the soft rustling of the waving branches.
name: Arm of the Forest

desc:
The small winding path carefully twists and turns, navigating through the maze
of great boles that crowd here. The canopy above is not very dense but still
blots out most of the light, creating a moving display of shadow and green as
the stars and the sun move in their eternal dance across the sky. From within
the far reaches of the forest, you hear the faint song of birds mixed with the
happy babbling of the small forest stream lying just east and north of you.
name: A Winding Path

desc:
As the stream continues towards the Celebrant, the ever-accompanying path turns
and crosses the flowing water. White stepping-stones have been placed in the
current to ease the passage of any traveller. Vanishing into the dense forest,
the trail runs on west-east, while the stream runs swiftly southward into a
small crack, vanishing underground.
name: Fordable Stream

desc:
The small path runs east and west, bordering the dense wood just to the north.
From the south, you can hear the Celebrant rushing by in its stony bed, and you
can barely discern the flowing water through the sparse woods. Eastwards, the
forest opens up, allowing the passage of a wide road, and to the west the path
crosses a small stream that happily flows southward on its quest to join greater
waters.
name: A Forest Trail

desc:
Beginning just atop the sloping bank of the Celebrant, this broad and paved road
runs in a straight line north, towards the fair city of the Galadhrim. Standing
dense at the edge of the road, the towering mellyrn whisper softly as the wind
flows through their boughs and the faint noise of the Celebrant echoes among
them.
name: Forest Road

desc:
Within the branches of this great tree, the elves have built a small wooden
platform. Here live those of the Galadhrim who are in charge of the stables and
the rope bridges across the Celebrant. The flet is spartanly furnished and
contains only a small table and a few sleeping mats. A small wooden ladder is
placed near the bole of the tree, to allow easy passage up and down the tree.
name: Small Flet

desc:
A line of low shrubbery and other vegetation borders the forest here, southwards
blending to the low, dry grass. In the north the massive, cool and shady forest,
yet but a tiny area of the huge Golden Wood, stands majestically. The air seems
aglow with a faint golden shine.
name: On the Fringes of the Forest

desc:
The path continues on its east-west course, making its way through the
undergrowth around the thick silver-grey trees. The higher mellyrn shadow the
forest floor, but in the golden glow under the mallorn branches many plants and
flowers flourish. From the northwest you can hear the roaring of the river as it
rushes on its course through the forest.
name: Path in the Mallorn Forest

desc:
The steep, rocky river bank is littered with debris. Branches and splinters of
wood lie scattered all about. The roaring river sprays white foam and bursts of
water onto the naked rock, making it slippery and wet. The roaring of the river
is deafening and the thunderous noise drowns out all other sounds. Higher up the
slope, the great mellyrn stand towering towards the sky, blocking your view.
name: River Bank

desc:
The steep river bank is full of branches and debris. A few smaller trees have
managed to creep down to the riverside, but the taller, wiser mellyrn stand
farther from the river, packed densely on the upper reaches of the slope.
Blending with the sound of the rushing water, the whispers of the golden forest
surround and envelope you.
name: Steep River Bank

desc:
The steep grade of the river bank has not prevented the trees from growing at
will here. The growth is not particularly dense, nor is it of any great height,
but the trees still seem to thrive. Farther south the woods grow dense and high
as the land once more becomes rather flat.
name: Wooded River Bank

desc:
The riverbank is less steep here and cluttered with small rocks and boulders and
the occasional debris floating ashore from the Celebrant. The few mellyrn that
stand silently on the bank are not as tall, but still give the impression of
peace and solemnity, projecting an aura of strength.
name: Bank of the Celebrant

desc:
Standing rather thin, the mellyrn here have managed to grow to a stunning
height, climbing high up towards the skies. Light shines playfully among the
branches and the foliage is lit up by the glow of the stars and the sun. The
towering trees are ancient and solemn and they stand silent, forever watching
and guarding the realm of Lothlorien.
name: Towering Forest

desc:
Towering high and proud, these great trees strive towards the sky, forever
reaching and yearning for the light. Standing rather sparsely, they have given
each other ample room for growth and strength, thus allowing to reach enormous
height. Even though the mallorn trees are very tall, they allow the light of the
heavens to fall upon the forest soil, letting many smaller bushes and shrubs
flower among the great silvery boles.
name: The Glorious Mellyrn

desc:
Here the forest is comprised mostly of mellyrn. Far in the northeast, behind a
thicker row of trees, the sounds of a river rushing through its rocky channel
can be heard. Here the forest is open and easy to travel through. The effect of
Lothlorien can be felt even here, such is the power it radiates; yet this effect
seems to dwindle to the southeast, where the forest thins and appears less
densely populated.
name: Spacious Forest

desc:
A mix of mellyrn and smaller, ordinary trees grows here on the border of the
Golden Wood. Although it seems that this area of the forest is not travelled
very often, closer inspection of the undergrowth reveals a small path travelling
north into the forest.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
The trees do not grow densely packed here, but leave plenty of room for other
plant life and gradually thin out to the south. Shrubbery and moss dominates the
landscape, but progress is fairly comfortable. To the west the silvery and
golden mellyrn can be seen growing on the border of the Golden Wood.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
Despite the dry climate various plant species seem to thrive on these open
plains. Some signs of wildlife are visible as well, the foot-long grass and
shrubs providing ample shelter and nourishment for many animals in the area. A
dried streambed can be seen to the south.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Almost completely crumbled, the channel of an ancient stream is still visible
here, although instead of water it is now filled by low shrubbery. Southward the
channel crosses an old path, pushing farther through a valley. On the old river
banks tufts of green grass line the course of the channel.
name: Old Streambed

desc:
A faint trail runs here, diving into the dried channel and crossing the
streambed, which is quite shallow here. It seems that this is an ancient ford
over the stream. Though the trail here is probably very seldom used it is not
overgrown at all; the grass grows very sparsely here for some reason.
name: Old Ford

desc:
It seems that a farm once stood here, as the stony foundations of several
buildings can still be seen even though nature seems to be taking over. Grass
and low shrubbery covers everything; the walls have crumbled long ago and the
interiors of the foundations are filled with dirt. The lands around the farm now
seem bare and infertile and not suitable for cultivation at all. The farm was
probably abandonded long ago.
name: Ruined Farm

desc:
The dusty path climbs the steep hillside at this place, ascending from the
dried-up ford in the west towards the hills in the east. The path is faint and
seems to be rarely trodden nowadays. Perhaps it served traders and travellers
better once, when the times were better and the valley green and cultivated.
name: Steep Path

desc:
This grassy area slopes upwards slightly to the east, nearing a hilly area. To
the west an old riverbed can be made out; it appears to be dry now. The land
here is covered with wildlife and greenery and seems like a very lively place
despite the lack of tending.
name: Sloping Grasslands

desc:
Passage through the light shrubbery is not difficult. The bushes have small,
narrow leaves and some seem to grow small thorns, perhaps as a way of warding
off small animals. The flora here seems to thrive in its environment. The ground
is uneven and criss-crossed by plant roots.
name: Sparse Bushes

desc:
A thicket of bushes here, a tangle of branches there, yet mostly this forest is
quite light and easily lets a traveller through. Soft clumps of bushes grow
around, their leaves showing many vivid hues of green. The forest floor is
covered by a layer of fallen leaves and other debris.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
A smooth, grassy field borders the edge of the forest in the north. Many kinds
of plants make up a uniform carpet of grass here, dotted only by an occasional
patch of shrubbery. No flowers appear to grow here, but the air carries a sweet
scent from the direction of the forest.
name: Level Grasslands

desc:
Not many mellyrn grow here, and other trees take over instead. Though they are
ordinary, broad-leaved trees they seem strangely tall and healthy. A feeling of
health seems to rest in this forest, and a tired traveller could find peace and
refreshment from the mere atmosphere of these woods.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
Here, close to the heart of Lothlorien, you can feel the agelessness of the
forest. The tall mellyrn standing all about reach high, striving towards the
sky, almost as if trying the reach the stars. Solemn and silent, they only pass
on an occasional whisper as the wind carefully rocks the enchanted forest.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
The forest stands rather sparse here, allowing for a light and colourful scene,
the light passing down through the golden canopy above to spill across the
forest floor. The sparkling play of shadow and light creates dancing shapes of
green, gold and grey, moving playfully among the tall grey trunks of the
towering mellyrn. From within the forest, you can hear the song of nightingales
as well as the whispering of the wind through the trees. Just north and west of
here, the ground begins to slope gently down towards the river Celebrant, which
can just barely be heard through the woods.
name: The Golden Forest

desc:
Sloping downwards towards the beautiful river Celebrant, the bank is still not
too steep for the great trees of the enchanted forest. Tall grey mellyrn stand
sparsely lined, allowing the sound of the flowing water to travel farther into
the woods, where it blends with the soft whisper of the trees and the faint song
of hidden nightingales.
name: A Wooded Slope

desc:
A small clearing lies here, open to the sky above and encircled by the densely
growing mellyrn of the Golden Wood. The grass of the clearing is lush and of a
rich, deep green, though some small patches have been trampled with the hooves
of passing animals. In the soft soil, a few old hoofprints can be seen. Just
outside the clearing, among the great grey trees, stands a small wooden
structure. The small building consists of a roof on wooden pillars and a single
wall to shelter the horses from the soft rains that pour down on occasion.
name: The Stables

desc:
You are surrounded by trees, the towering great mellyrn eternally reaching
towards the sky. Immersed in shadow, the forest stands solemn and silent around
you. The thick undergrowth hinders any movement. Only an occasional whisper, as
a gentle breeze rocks the trees, disturbs the peace of the enchanted wood. No
matter which direction you gaze, the forest lies dense, expanding out toward the
horizon.
name: Within the Golden Wood

desc:
The grey trees stand tall here, shrouded in shadow as only few rays of light
permeate the dense canopy to create swirling displays of green and yellow. Even
so, the shadows are not dark and threatening, but rather grey-green, and provide
protection for the smaller creatures wishing to avoid the watchful eye. The soft
song of nightingales can be heard from within the deeper reaches of the forest,
as well as the soft whisper of the branches swaying gently in the wind.
name: Amid the Mellyrn

desc:
The ancient forest of the enchanted realm of Lothlorien, the Golden Wood, is
surrounding you, immersing you in soft shadow and the gentle whispers of the
mellyrn. Grey and proud they stand, towering towards the sky as a reminder of
the power and beauty dwelling within the forest borders.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The shrubbery in the northeast gives way to the low grass which carpets this
level and monotonous landscape. To the south some old ruins rise above the
grasslands, and in the distant northwest a dark line can be seen on the horizon;
the eaves of the Golden Wood of Lothlorien.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A path once ran through this area, but it is now very faint and almost
unmarkable. Foundations of an old building lie about; big blocks of stone lie
scattered around, and grass now covers everything. Among the overgrown ruins lie
pieces of metal and other debris. The destruction of this area must have been
violent indeed.
name: Grassy Ruins

desc:
Once perhaps a fertile field or a green pasture this area is now a barren, rocky
slope, growing just about nothing. The ground slopes downward slightly to the
west, forming a small valley in the distance. Large boulders and stones lie
about; some of them look as though they were once part of a man-made structure,
perhaps a quarry or corral. A broken shield lies among the rocky debris.
name: Ruined Fields

desc:
This square flet with level floor made of pale polished wood is wedged
precariously among the bare silver branches of a mallorn tree. Its surface is
open to sun and wind. A bundle of mesh ladder, heaped in its south-western
corner, looks like the only way in or out of here. The magnificent view over the
rooftop of the Golden Wood is breathtaking, but no detail can be ascertained of
anything moving below its thick protective cover.
name: An Exposed Talan

desc:
Under a fallen branch is a deep depression in which the fallen leaves of this
area of the forest have been accumulating. Combined with the water seeping in
from the nearby river, the fallen leaves of many seasons have decomposed into a
knee-deep, slick, smelly, rotting mess.
name: In a Foetid Pile

desc:
Running above the raging Celebrant, this small forest trail lies in the shadow
of the tall mellyrn towering at its border. The forest stand dense and dark, a
solemn wall of grey pillars supporting the overshadowing canopy of gold and
green. Soft and soothing, the song of the Celebrant blends with the soft
whispers of the woods creating a veil of background noise.
name: A Forest Trail

desc:
Running high upon the cliff that rises above the roaring water, this small trail
lies in between the river and forest, acting as a border. Bounded by the great
grey trunks of the mellyrn and the deep drop down to the raging Celebrant, this
is a place of great beauty as well as a place of danger.
name: Trail above the Celebrant

desc:
Here, on the southern bank of the great river Celebrant, the densely growing
mellyrn give way slightly, opening up to allow a landing. A large rock lies
immersed in water just off the shore, creating a small area of peace and
tranquility in the otherwise steadily flowing water. The powerful roar of the
water echoes among the trees, almost drowning out the soft whispers of the
forest and the singing of birds. Crossing the river is a slender rope, to which
a delicate ferry is attached.
name: A Landing

desc:
A small wooden construction rests firmly on the sloping bank here, reaching into
the current just far enough to create a pocket of solemn and tranquil water next
to the bank. It is not quite a pier, but can still serve as a landing for
boatsmen ferrying goods or travellers across. Close by the water, a tall tree
stands, almost as a sentinel, with a wooden ladder running up the trunk. A
slender rope can be seen running across the water to a landing on the other side
of the river. A ferry is attached to the rope.
name: Landing in the Forest

desc:
As the ravine bends, the water smashes into the rocky walls with incredible
force and large, jagged stones rise above the surface like pointing fingers,
ready to crush anything unwelcome. The roar of the water drowns out all other
sounds as the echoes within the ravine are hurled back and forth without
respite.
name: Raging Bend

desc:
Made of a pale timber and carved with exquisite care to resemble a large white
swan, this ferry is a delicate piece of art as well as an easy way to cross the
Celebrant. A slender rope is fastened on both the prow and the stern of the
craft, allowing anyone on either shore to manoeuvre the small ship to their
side. Long, slender poles of shiny wood lie in the ferry to allow easy crossing
of the waters. A small silver lamp hangs from the prow of the vessel to light it
should the stars and moon be covered in clouds.
name: On a White Swan
